name: Insertion sort
precondition: input list is finite and comparison is total
postcondition: output is sorted and preserves the input multiset
complexity_note: O(n^2) worst-case time and O(1) auxiliary space
responsible_use_note: formal sorting correctness does not validate downstream ranking criteria

name: Binary search
input_domain: sorted finite sequence and target value
precondition: sequence is sorted in nondecreasing order
postcondition: returns a valid target index or a missing result
complexity_note: O(log n) time and O(1) auxiliary space
responsible_use_note: requires explicit equality and ordering semantics

name: Merge sort
precondition: input list is finite and comparison is total
postcondition: output is sorted and preserves the input multiset
complexity_note: O(n log n) time and O(n) auxiliary space
responsible_use_note: efficient ordering does not justify what is being ordered

name: Breadth-first search
precondition: start vertex exists in graph and adjacency representation is valid
postcondition: all reachable vertices are discovered with shortest edge-count distances
termination_measure: number of undiscovered reachable vertices decreases
responsible_use_note: edge meaning determines whether reachability has real-world significance

name: Dijkstra shortest path
postcondition: distances from source are shortest under the given weight semantics
complexity_note: often O((V+E) log V) with a priority queue
responsible_use_note: shortest means only what the weight definition says it means

name: Heap insert
postcondition: output structure satisfies heap invariant and contains new item
termination_measure: distance from inserted item to root decreases during bubble-up
responsible_use_note: priority order may encode values or harms outside the formal heap property
